summary:
The hand-to-knee gait observed in patients with polio indicates weakness primarily affecting the quadriceps muscles. This leads to impaired ability to extend the hips while walking, characteristic of this condition. \boxed{C}
The summary concisely captures the essential point regarding the impact of weakened quadriceps on a patient’s gait and directly links it back to the correct answer choice provided by identifying "quadriceps" as the primary affected muscle group responsible for causing such symptoms in poliomyelitis cases.
